topic: CLI-fallback feasibility for the mk_skill_advisor MCP server (system-skill-advisor, 9 tools: advisor_recommend/rebuild/status/validate + skill_graph_scan/query/status/validate/propagate_enhances) — dual-stack scope: the MCP registration STAYS; the CLI is the resilience + universal surface. PREMISE (settled, do not relitigate): the spec-memory record at .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/027-xce-research-based-refinement/004-shared-infrastructure/001-mcp-to-cli-tool-transition/001-spec-memory-cli/000-spec-memory-cli-research/research/research.md (sections 1-14) established the generic CLI-over-daemon pattern — thin IPC client, auto-spawn via the existing launcher, exit map 0/1/64/69/75, subcommand codegen from the canonical registry, warm-only hook policy (~40-46ms p95 node start measured on this host), owner-lease single-writer discipline. READ IT AS INPUT. Your mission: investigate the mk_skill_advisor-SPECIFIC deltas only, with file:line or measured evidence; read-only bash timing measurements allowed, file mutation outside your artifact dir is not. Architecture anchors (verified): TOOL_DEFINITIONS in .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/tools/index.ts (~37-43, dispatchTool ~51-70); Zod schemas in mcp_server/schemas/advisor-tool-schemas.ts (~1-383, exportable — closest sibling to spec-memory codegen); launcher .opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs (bootstrap lock ~72, strict single-writer ~246, shares launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs); advisor-server.ts FS watcher (~86: graph-metadata auto-rebuild daemon); state: skill-graph.sqlite + skill-graph-daemon-lease.sqlite + compiled skill-graph.json via scripts/skill_graph_compiler.py; THE CENTRAL PRIOR-ART QUESTION: scripts/skill_advisor.py (3,642 lines) is the documented Gate-2 fallback CLI — full advisor_recommend coverage via a native-bridge probe (~2795-2900) falling back to a DIVERGENT Python scorer, partial --health/--validate-only coverage, ZERO coverage for advisor_rebuild and 4 of 5 skill_graph tools; batch modes, --force-local/--force-native, thresholds 0.8/0.35. Incident context: six orphaned mk-skill-advisor launchers were observed on this host — the CLI-spawn lifecycle question is live for THIS system specifically. The seed register — one focus per iteration, 10 forced iterations: KQ1 parity matrix, all 9 tools: STATELESS vs STATE-DAEMON classification, CLI mapping per tool, daemon-required vs direct-read paths. KQ2 daemon-dependency audit: what dies per architecture — FS watcher auto-rebuild, daemon-lease sqlite, in-process prompt cache, telemetry sink, anything resident. KQ3 affordance transfer: which spec-memory answers port verbatim and which do not — Zod schemas here ARE exportable, so confirm the codegen path is the closest sibling and name any divergence. KQ4 prior-art deep-dive (CENTRAL): build the skill_advisor.py coverage matrix per tool; QUANTIFY Python-vs-native scorer divergence empirically (run BOTH paths on identical prompts read-only — e.g. --force-local vs --force-native on 5-10 representative prompts — and measure recommendation/score deltas); explain the native-bridge probe mechanics; deliver a terminal verdict: reconcile, supersede, or coexist, with rationale and consequences for Gate-2 callers. KQ5 long-running ops: advisor_rebuild and skill_graph_scan duration profile + the skill_graph_compiler.py build chain — per-call CLI vs async job semantics. KQ6 integration-surface map MEASURED: exact files/counts — UserPromptSubmit hooks (claude/gemini/codex), codex prompt-wrapper fallback, OpenCode plugin + plugin bridge (mk-skill-advisor.js, mk-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs), doctor:skill-advisor + skill-budget routes, deep-loop callers, opencode.json. KQ7 hook-latency fit (SHARPEST HERE): the advisor brief sits on the prompt-submit critical path in every runtime — measure the current brief generation path cost and bound what a CLI shell-out adds; state the warm-only policy verdict for this system. KQ8 dual-stack coexistence + races + THE ORPHAN INCIDENT: root-cause the six-orphaned-launchers class from the launcher source (which lifecycle path leaks), and specify the reaping guarantee a CLI-spawn path needs. KQ9 risk register + named design deltas (D-series) an implementation phase must absorb, each with file/mechanism/acceptance. KQ10 verdict synthesis: go/no-go, architecture pick (including whether the CLI subsumes or wraps skill_advisor.py), bottom-up effort, explicit inheritance list, and corrections to any spec-memory assumption that did not transfer. Every KQ must end terminally answered with evidence; write per-iteration findings and a final verdict-shaped research.md in your artifact dir.
